rationale: Y is the only candidate that engages the PR's central, most-repeated reviewer concern (endpoint restrictions being resolved from the auto-detected pipeline instead of the post-deploy-override pipeline) and correctly recognizes it as fixed via get_pipeline_config's ordering plus the regression test — exactly what amy-why-3459/hsliuustc0106 asked for. X never mentions this at all. X compensates with more numerous, highly actionable line-specific fixes (trust_remote_code default, missing try/except, cache poisoning), but one claim ('hf_config = None ... never read') is factually wrong since it IS read on the exception path, and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict miscalibrates severity relative to what actual reviewers treated as non-blocking. Y's findings (error-response fragility, else-branch skipping UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES, cache-fixture scope) are all grounded and verifiable against the shown diff, and its APPROVE verdict matches the real outcome.
